examples.title: Algorithm: Lexical Analysis
examples.description: Demonstrates the process of breaking up source code into tokens
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LexicalAnalysis
INPUT: sourceCode
OUTPUT: tokens

BEGIN
 tokens = []
 WHILE hasMoreCharacters(sourceCode) DO
 IF isWhitespace(currentCharacter) THEN
 skipWhitespace()
 ELSE IF isIdentifier(currentCharacter) THEN
 tokens.add(identifyIdentifier())
 ELSE IF isOperator(currentCharacter) THEN
 tokens.add(identifyOperator())
 END IF
 END WHILE
 RETURN tokens
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic steps involved in lexical analysis, including skipping whitespace, identifying identifiers and operators, and returning a list of tokens.

examples.title: Python: Syntax Analysis
examples.description: Demonstrates the process of parsing tokens into an abstract syntax tree
examples.code:
import pyparsing as pp

# Define the grammar
identifier = pp.Word(pp.alphas, pp.alphanums)
operator = pp.oneOf('+ - * /')
expression = pp.Forward()
expression << identifier + pp.ZeroOrMore(operator + expression)

# Parse the tokens
source_code = 'a + b * c'
tokens = pp.parseString(expression, source_code)

# Print the abstract syntax tree
print(tokens)
examples.explanation: This Python code uses the pyparsing library to define a grammar and parse a string of tokens into an abstract syntax tree.

examples.title: C: Intermediate Code Generation
examples.description: Demonstrates the process of generating intermediate code from an abstract syntax tree
examples.explanation: This C code defines an abstract syntax tree structure and generates intermediate code from it by recursively traversing the tree and printing the node values.
